description:
When mounting with -o backend=fskit, the daemon segfaults on the first directory access. Crash is EXC_BAD_ACCESS at the value of the readdir filler argument (varies per run: 0x120a8, 0x3ff800), which is clearly not a function pointer.

Initial hypothesis was libfuse 3.16 vs 3.18 ABI skew. Porting bindings to 3.18 (separate commit) fixes real latent bugs (fuse_file_info::fh offset, fuse_config tail) but does NOT fix this crash.

Root cause is macFUSE's Apple-flavored extensions to fuse_operations — see /usr/local/include/fuse3/fuse.h and fuse_common.h:

  #if defined(__APPLE__) && FUSE_DARWIN_ENABLE_EXTENSIONS  // default on
  #define FUSE_DARWIN_EXTEND_OPERATION(name, vanilla, darwin) \
      __typeof__(darwin) name;

This silently rewrites the C struct on macOS so that:

  * getattr takes  struct fuse_darwin_attr*  (not struct stat*)
  * statfs  takes  struct statfs*  (not struct statvfs*)
  * setxattr/getxattr have an extra uint32_t options arg
  * readdir's filler is fuse_darwin_fill_dir_t (sig differs from fuse_fill_dir_t)
  * utimens takes  const struct timespec tv[3]  (not [2])

AND adds Apple-only fields that shift every later offset:

  * setattr               after getattr
  * chflags / setvolname / monitor   after lseek, before statx

Net effect on Apple: 47 fields, 376 bytes — vs. our 43-field, 344-byte vanilla struct. So when libfuse looks up readdir at its expected offset (208 on Darwin), it picks up the wrong slot in our table, or — more likely — the FSKit dispatcher uses Darwin-extended signatures that pass non-function-pointer tokens in the filler slot.

Three workaround paths, in increasing scope:

  1. Skip ops.readdir registration when detect_macfuse_backend returns FSKit.MacFuse, let LOOKUP-per-entry handle directory enumeration. Cheap to try; might be enough.
  2. Add the Apple-only fields to fuse_operations (setattr, chflags, setvolname, monitor) so subsequent fields land at the right offsets. May be enough on its own if the readdir filler is just at the wrong location.
  3. Switch to libfuse's low-level API (fuse_lowlevel.h), which doesn't go through the Darwin-extended path.
title: fuse_operations Darwin signatures missing (FSKit readdir crash)
